title: AWS resource tags should have valid format
description: AWS resource tags are used for organization, automation, cost allocation, and access control. Tags with invalid formats (e.g., empty keys, reserved prefixes, or invalid characters) can break automation, cause billing errors, and hinder resource management. Validating tag formats ensures consistent resource governance and compliance.
message: Resource tag format is invalid. Ensure all tags use valid keys and values.
impact: Invalid tags can disrupt automation, cost tracking, and access control, leading to operational inefficiencies and compliance issues.
recommendation: Use only valid tag keys and values as per AWS guidelines. Avoid reserved prefixes (e.g., 'aws:'), empty keys, and unsupported characters. Implement tag validation in CI/CD pipelines.

noncompliant.description: Tags use an empty key, a reserved prefix, and invalid characters, violating AWS tag format rules.

compliant.description: All tags use valid keys and values, following AWS best practices.

devsecops.security: Consistent tagging supports access control, incident response, and resource tracking.
devsecops.automation: Automated tag validation in CI/CD prevents deployment of misconfigured resources.
devsecops.compliance: Accurate tagging is required for cost allocation and regulatory reporting.

why_is_this_an_issue: Improperly formatted tags can break automation, hinder resource discovery, and cause compliance or billing errors.
how_to_fix: Review all resource tags. Ensure keys are non-empty, do not use reserved prefixes, and contain only allowed characters. Enforce tag policies via automation.
